coverage: representative slice of the shipped 229-quality vocabulary: 48 qualities (24 curated + 24 extra), 2 samples each
improvement_over_previous: Adding a tautology filter (a quality never pairs with an impact echoing its own word/root) ELIMINATED the prior tautologies and lifted overall 0.89 -> 0.92, appropriateness 0.90 -> 0.95, per-quality accuracy 0.83 -> 0.96.
verdict: Tautologies are gone: perspective now pairs with 'caught the problems early', honesty with 'made things clearer', insight with 'caught the problems early', and no impact echoes its quality's word or root. Appropriateness rose and relevance is perfect. Variety (0.78) is now the sole ceiling, held back by ~8 byte-identical sample pairs.
weaknesses: ~8 byte-identical sample pairs remain (generosity, honesty, leadership, optimism, flexibility, impartiality, brilliance, intelligence) — within-quality variety still imperfect at temperature 0.85.
a few stock impacts ('made everyone feel welcome', 'caught the problems early') are reused across many qualities — appropriate but under-differentiated.
raw model output is all-lowercase (the web app prettifies for display).
judge_progression: 0.83 (coarse families) -> 0.88 (split families) -> 0.89 (enriched pool, variety 0.66->0.79) -> 0.92 (tautology filter). Each pass fixed what the prior flagged, re-measured.
note: LLM-as-judge on the actual shipped model; the judge's stricter score is preferred over the ~100% deterministic appropriateness. Not a capability-class claim.
